title: World Cup 2026 Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: With 81 days to the June 11 kickoff, the FIFA World Cup 2026 enters its final qualification phase. The intercontinental playoffs on March 26 and 31 will be hosted at Mexican venues (Estadio Guadalajara and Estadio Monterrey), with DR Congo, Iraq, New Caledonia, Bolivia, Jamaica, and Suriname competing for two final World Cup berths. Simultaneously, 16 UEFA teams compete for 4 European spots on the same dates. The 48-team, 104-match tournament will span 16 host cities across the USA, Mexico, and Canada, with the final scheduled for July 19 at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey.
